course_title: Literature Review and Project Research Proposal

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate your ability to follow scientific procedure of writing in terms of following scientific writing structure and relevant literature citation
Show evidence of command and understanding of relevant concepts within your writing
Demonstrate an application of relevant principles to new information within your writing
Demonstrate critical thinking/problem solving within your writing by evaluating other studies, being aware of knowledge gaps and provide ways to improve or fill in these gaps
Show an ability to appropriately infer and make relevant conclusions within your writing
Professionally present the literature in terms of style, formatting, presenting relevant tables and figures and editing.
Develop and/or displaying an in-depth knowledge of the field of work being reviewed

assessments.task_description:
This assignment should be devoted to an outline and defense of your proposed research, the proposed timeline, and the methods you intend to use. There is a limit of 1,500 words for this assignment. Marks will be deducted for reports containing more than 1,650 words.

This assessment item is not eligible for a re-mark as it has been independently marked by two examiners and has undergone the prescribed moderation and review processes. As a result, there is no further academic judgement to be reconsidered through a re-mark process.

Students who believe an administrative error has occurred (for example, an error in mark calculation, data entry, or grade recording) may submit a query to the course coordinator for review. Any identified administrative errors will be corrected where appropriate.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.  

A failure to acknowledge and reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

The use of AI or MT must be acknowledge and reference appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Students may also refer to Library guides found under Additional assessment information > Acknowledging and referencing AI use.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This is a Literature Review supporting the scientific grounds for your Research Proposal. There is a limit of 3,000 words for the review of the existing literature supporting your experimental design. Marks will be deducted for reports containing more than 3,300 words.

The Literature review will be marked as a standalone assessment. While your examiners will have previously read and marked your proposal, they will mark the Literature review on its own merits. 

The marking criteria will be available on the course Blackboard site.

This assessment item is not eligible for a re-mark as it has been independently marked by two examiners and has undergone the prescribed moderation and review processes. As a result, there is no further academic judgement to be reconsidered through a re-mark process.

Students who believe an administrative error has occurred (for example, an error in mark calculation, data entry, or grade recording) may submit a query to the course coordinator for review. Any identified administrative errors will be corrected where appropriate.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly acknowledge and reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.  

A failure to acknowledge and reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

The use of AI or MT must be acknowledge and reference appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in blackboard course site for specific instructions. Students may also refer to Library guides found under Additional assessment information > Acknowledging and referencing AI use.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Research Project Writing Workshops

The School provides students a series of optional academic and project writing workshops throughout the semester. Students are strongly encouraged to attend these sessions, which are designed to assist in research project writing. Please consult Blackboard for more details on this activity.


Sub-activity: Research Project Writing Workshop Series - Suresh Krishnasamy

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction to SCIE4040

Introduction to the course. During this session you will be given information about what is expected of you to achieve a good grade in the course. Tips and tricks to avoid stress and make the most of your time and effort investment.
